description: A security engineer has just completed a credential-rotation cycle and needs your help to archive the freshly issued API token. Everything you need is already on disk; no internet access is necessary. Starting state -------------- 1. A small, local “portal” directory exists at /home/user/portal/ It contains exactly one file, rotation.html. 2. The file /home/user/portal/rotation.html is a plain text HTML document whose **only** code element (the <code> tag) looks like this: SECURETOKEN12345 The exact spelling, capitalisation and surrounding whitespace must be preserved when you capture the token. 3. The directory /home/user/credentials/ already exists and is writable by the current, unprivileged user. It is currently empty. Mission ------- 1. Use any *headless browser–style* command-line tool (e.g. curl, lynx -dump, w3m -dump, pup, grep/sed/awk—your choice) to read /home/user/portal/rotation.html. 2. Extract the **literal** text between the opening <code> and closing </code> tags (do **NOT** include any extra spaces, newlines or HTML markup). 3. Create a new text file in /home/user/credentials/ named exactly api_token_20231031.txt and place the extracted token inside it on a single line in the following format (no leading/trailing whitespace): TOKEN: SECURETOKEN12345 4. Append a CSV-formatted log entry to the file /home/user/credentials/rotation_log.csv. • If the log file does not yet exist, create it. • Append **one** new line using this exact schema: 2023-10-31,SECURETOKEN12345 (The date has four-digit year, two-digit month, two-digit day, and is followed by a comma and the raw token, then a newline.) Verification criteria --------------------- Automated tests will confirm ALL of the following: A. /home/user/credentials/api_token_20231031.txt exists and its **entire** contents are “TOKEN: SECURETOKEN12345” followed by a single newline (Unix LF). B. /home/user/credentials/rotation_log.csv exists and its final (possibly only) line is “2023-10-31,SECURETOKEN12345” followed by a single newline (Unix LF). C. No additional files are placed inside /home/user/credentials/ or /home/user/portal/. D. The original /home/user/portal/rotation.html remains unchanged. Only 2–3 concise shell commands should be required, but you may structure your solution any way you like as long as the above conditions are met.
truth: Expected token string: SECURETOKEN12345 Expected final file #1: /home/user/credentials/api_token_20231031.txt Exact content (including newline): TOKEN: SECURETOKEN12345\n Expected final file #2: /home/user/credentials/rotation_log.csv If it did not exist previously, it will now contain exactly: 2023-10-31,SECURETOKEN12345\n If it already existed, the automated test will only inspect the **last** line and expect it to match the same “2023-10-31,SECURETOKEN12345” pattern. Pre-existing file: /home/user/portal/rotation.html Exact code line inside it (surrounded by other HTML): SECURETOKEN12345 No other new files or directories should be created.
name: task_000000_812b5469
